checklist.entry: Distinguish uploading a Worker version from deploying that version to traffic.
Record whether a change uses immediate deployment, saved version, gradual deployment or version override.
Do not claim storage resource changes are tracked by Worker versions.
Use deployment metadata and version IDs when reporting production evidence.
Use gradual deployments for risk-controlled rollout of critical Workers.
Monitor errors, exceptions and performance differences during rollout.
Use version overrides only when the target version is in the current deployment.
For Durable Objects, treat migrations as atomic and avoid assuming rollback to versions before the migration.
Deploy Durable Object migrations independently from unrelated code changes where possible.
Use a scheduled handler for Cron Triggers.
Cron Triggers execute on UTC time.
When a Worker is managed with Wrangler, manage Cron Triggers through Wrangler configuration.
Expect Cron Trigger changes to take several minutes to propagate globally.
Test local scheduled handlers through the Wrangler scheduled handler route.
